title: Ising anyons in the $SU(2)_2$ Chern--Simons theory
abstract: The present work is motivated by the statement that the Ising minimal model $\mathcal{M}(4,3)$ is equivalent, at the level of observables, to the $SU(2)_2$ Chern--Simons theory. At first glance, however, these two theories appear to differ substantially. For instance, the number of irreducible highest-weight representations does not match the number of Ising anyons. For tensor products of low degree, these discrepancies are examined in this work. While representation structure differs, it does not affect the observables underlying topological quantum computation algorithms.
summary: This theoretical work rigorously compares the algebraic structures of the Ising minimal model and the $SU(2)_2$ Chern-Simons theory. By analyzing tensor product decompositions and fusion rules, the authors resolve apparent mathematical contradictions, confirming that the two descriptions yield consistent physical observables for Ising anyons.
main_problem: The paper resolves apparent algebraic contradictions between the fusion/braiding rules of the Ising minimal model and the representation theory of the $SU(2)_2$ Chern-Simons theory.
main_result: The tensor product decomposition rules for the quantum algebra $U_q(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$ at roots of unity successfully reproduce the known fusion and braiding rules of the Ising anyons, confirming the equivalence at the level of observables.
method: The authors compare results derived from two formalisms: Operator Product Expansions (OPEs) for the CFT side and the representation theory of the quantum group $U_q(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$ for the Chern-Simons side.
model_or_system: The study concerns the mathematical equivalence between the Ising minimal model $\mathcal{M}(4,3)$ (a 2D CFT with $c=1/2$) and the $SU(2)_2$ Chern-Simons theory, which describes Ising anyons.
key_observables: Wilson loop expectation values $\langle W_K^R angle$, fusion matrices ($F$), and braiding matrices ($R$).
important_parameters: The deformation parameter $q$ is set to a root of unity, specifically $q = \exp(\pi i/4)$.
main_assumptions: The comparison is restricted to low-degree tensor products, and the resolution of contradictions relies on the fact that representations with zero quantum dimension do not contribute to observable quantities.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the Ising fusion rules, the structure of the 52 knot in different closures, and the transition matrices ($F^{\otimes\otimes\otimes}$) governing tensor product decompositions.
paper_structure: The paper proceeds by establishing the algebraic structures for both theories, calculating specific observables (like Wilson loops), and systematically comparing the tensor product decompositions ($\otimes^{\otimes\otimes\otimes}$) to show consistency.
why_interesting: The deep connection between topological quantum field theories (Chern-Simons) and exactly solvable models (CFTs) is fundamental to understanding topological quantum computation, which is a key area of theoretical physics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
